name: Orange Pork Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Mixed fresh vegetables (choose from: green pepper, red pepper, snow peas, carrots, green onions, mushrooms, or onions)
1/2 cup Unsalted cashew bits (or cashew halves)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the orange juice, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, soy sauce, garlic, sherry, ginger, and orange peel in a small bowl.
2. Add pork; marinate for one hour.
3. Drain the pork; reserve the marinade.
4. Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
5. Add the pork; stir-fry for 3 minutes, or until lightly browned.
6. Add vegetables; stir-fry for 3 to 5 minutes until tender-crisp.
7. Combine the cornstarch with the marinade and add it to the pan. Stir until thickened.
8. Add cashews; cook for an additional minute.
9. Serve immediately over hot cooked rice.
1 pound package frozen stir-fry vegetables, thawed, may be substituted for fresh vegetables, if desired.
Each serving provides:
463 calories
40.2 g of protein
11.2 g of fat
50.7 g of carbohydrate
4.8 g of dietary fiber
78 mg of cholesterol
1378 mg of sodium
Source: The Many Nationalities of Rice
